item: Calculate compound interest on $10,000 at 5% over 20 years.

text:
PLAN: I will calculate the compound interest using the formula A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt), where P is the principal amount, r is the annual interest rate, n is the number of times that interest is compounded per year, and t is the time the money is invested for. I will then subtract the principal from the total amount to find the compound interest earned.
ACTION: I will use the provided values: P = $10,000, r = 0.05, n = 1 (compounded annually), and t = 20 years. Plugging these into the formula, A = 10000(1 + 0.05/1)^(1*20) = 10000(1.05)^20. This calculates to A = $26,532.98. The compound interest earned is A - P = $26,532.98 - $10,000 = $16,532
